notes: PREPARATION FOR THE MINOR One of the following courses in creative practice: THEA 30: Introduction to Dance Theory and Technique THEA 36: Introduction to Dance Making Plus one course in cross-cultural techniques, chosen from the following: THEA 31A: Dance Studio I: Asian or Asian Diasporic Practice THEA 31B: Dance Studio I: Ballet THEA 31C: Dance Studio I: Contemporary Dance Theory and Technique THEA 37: African Dance THEA 80R: Bollywood Dance and Culture in India and Indian Diaspora THEA 80U: Everybody Dance Now! THEA 80Z: Indian Dance The following fundamentals course: THEA 50: Fundamentals of Theater Production Transfer students may petition to have equivalent lower-division courses taken at other schools count toward lower-division minor requirements and should consult with the Theater Arts academic advisor as early as possible regarding this possibility. Please visit the Performance, Play & Design website to learn more about this minor.

UC Santa Cruz students may choose to complete one or more minors, provided they can finish all required coursework within their maximum enrollment terms and allowable credits. While a minor will appear on a student's official transcript, it will not be listed on their diploma. Pursuing a minor is a personal decision that should align with a student's educational and career goals. Students do not apply for minors when submitting their application to UC Santa Cruz. After enrolling, transfer students interested in pursuing a minor should contact the department offering the minor for guidance.
